🚁 Farm Drones: How to Choose, Where to Fly, What They Cost

Why Use Drones on Your Farm?

Drones offer UK farmers: - Crop monitoring – spot disease, pests, irrigation issues early - Livestock tracking – find animals, monitor herd health - Field mapping – create accurate boundary maps, measure areas - Spraying – targeted pesticide/fertilizer application - Insurance documentation – aerial photos for claims

Legal Requirements (CAA Regulations)

Before flying, you need: 1. Operator ID (£10/year) – register as drone operator 2. Flyer ID (free, online test) – prove you know rules 3. Insurance – public liability minimum £1m 4. No-fly zones – avoid airports, military bases, crowded areas

Key rules: - Max height: 120m (400ft) - Keep visual line of sight - Don't fly over uninvolved people - Stay 150m from built-up areas

Best Drones by Farm Size

Small Farms (Under 50 hectares)

| Drone | Price | Best For | |-------|-------|----------| | DJI Mini 4 Pro | £1,000 | Crop scouting, mapping | | Autel EVO Nano+ | £800 | Livestock monitoring |

Medium Farms (50-200 hectares)

| Drone | Price | Best For | |-------|-------|----------| | DJI Mavic 3M | £4,500 | Multispectral crop analysis | | SenseFly eBee X | £15,000 | Professional mapping |

Large Farms (200+ hectares)

| Drone | Price | Best For | |-------|-------|----------| | DJI Agras T40 | £25,000 | Crop spraying | | PrecisionHawk Lancaster | £35,000 | Enterprise solutions |

Total Cost Breakdown

Entry Level: - Drone: £800-1,500 - Software: £50/month - Training: £200 - Insurance: £150/year - Total Year 1: £2,400

Professional: - Drone: £15,000-35,000 - Software: £200/month - Training: £500 - Insurance: £500/year - Total Year 1: £18,900

ROI Calculator

Time saved per year: - Crop scouting: 40 hours → 5 hours (saves 35h) - Boundary mapping: 16 hours → 2 hours (saves 14h) - Livestock checks: 20 hours → 8 hours (saves 12h) - Total time saved: 61 hours/year

At £25/hour: £1,525/year value

Payback period: - Entry drone: 1.6 years - Professional: 12+ years (but more capability)

90-Day Startup Plan

Month 1: - Register with CAA (Operator/Flyer ID) - Buy insurance - Purchase entry-level drone

Month 2: - Practice flying in open fields - Learn software basics - First crop scouting missions

Month 3: - Integrate into regular workflow - Document improvements - Consider upgrading if valuable

Resources

- CAA Drone Registration: register-drones.caa.co.uk - Drone Code: dronesafe.uk - NFU Drone Guide: nfuonline.com - Training: UAVAir, Coptrz (UK providers)

Last updated: 26 February 2026
